source_basis: EIA Today in Energy 2025-06-16 figure data gives 20.26 mb/d 2024 Hormuz oil traffic; EIA STEO accessed 2026-07-06 May production cut >11 mb/d and near-term effective-closure forecast
notes: Low equals EIA reported May Middle East crude production reduction versus pre-conflict; base equals baseline less 2.6 mb/d spare Saudi/UAE bypass and 2.6 mb/d assumed delayed/stocked/priority flow; high equals full 2024 oil-flow exposure. Needs reconciliation with AIS cargo mix and actual bypass utilization.

source_basis: EIA Today in Energy 2025-06-16 estimates 2.6 mb/d spare Saudi/UAE bypass capacity; IEA topic page accessed 2026-07-06 estimates 3.5-5.5 mb/d available capacity; kmz.2 route review records Saudi East-West and UAE ADCOP constraints
notes: Use as an offset sensitivity, not supply created. Nameplate capacity is materially higher than spare/usable capacity, and Saudi East-West may already be heavily utilized.

source_basis: IEA Strait of Hormuz page gives about 15 mb/d crude through Hormuz in 2025; EIA Today in Energy 2025-06-16 gives Saudi 5.48 mb/d in 2024 and EIA bypass capacity estimate 2.6 mb/d
notes: Base subtracts EIA's 2.6 mb/d spare Saudi/UAE bypass capacity from IEA's 2025 crude baseline; low applies a rough proportional share of EIA May production reduction. Treat as model estimate, not observed cargo loss.

source_basis: IEA Middle East and Global Energy Markets accessed 2026-07-06; Gulf refined product exports 3.3 mb/d in 2025; nearly 3 mb/d regional refining capacity shut
notes: IEA figure is Gulf exports, not necessarily all through Hormuz. Base assumes most but not all exports constrained because some products may move via Red Sea/Fujairah or draw inventories.

source_basis: IEA Middle East and Global Energy Markets accessed 2026-07-06; Gulf producers exported 1.5 mb/d LPG in 2025
notes: IEA does not fully split route/path in public text. Keep separate from refined products to avoid double-counting with petroleum liquids totals unless final taxonomy defines LPG treatment.

source_basis: IEA Middle East and Global Energy Markets accessed 2026-07-06; over 110 bcm transited Hormuz in 2025 and >300 mcm/d supply loss since 2026-03-01
notes: There are no practical alternative routes for Qatar/UAE LNG. Base equals IEA reported loss >300 mcm/d; high allows rounding above that observed level.

source_basis: IEA topic page accessed 2026-07-06; EIA STEO accessed 2026-07-06
notes: IEA says observed global oil stocks declined 3.8 mb/d on average since war start; EIA forecasts inventory draws of 6.3 mb/d in 2Q26 and 7.6 mb/d in 3Q26. Use as consistency check, not a product-specific disruption estimate.
